papers.title: Identifying connector versus full-trip users of a microtransit service
papers.summary: Rivera et al. conducted a study in a mid-sized US metro focusing on how riders use a microtransit zone, using survey data and regression, and find that connector trips were associated with younger, transit-dependent riders.

papers.title: Fleet sizing and rebalancing for on-demand shared rides
papers.summary: Okafor et al. conducted a simulation study focusing on fleet sizing for on-demand services using an agent-based model, and find that proactive rebalancing was associated with shorter average wait times.

papers.title: Free-floating carsharing membership and car ownership
papers.summary: Bianchi et al. conducted a study in several European cities focusing on free-floating carsharing using panel survey data, and find that sustained membership was associated with reduced household car ownership.

papers.title: Demand-responsive transit during the pandemic: a service-change review
papers.summary: Haddad et al. conducted a review focusing on demand-responsive service adjustments during COVID-19 using operator records, and find that flexible services were associated with faster ridership recovery than fixed routes.
